overview.tagline: A vibrant, multicultural community on the Palisades
overview.description: Palisades Park is one of the most densely populated and ethnically diverse boroughs in Bergen County. Situated atop the Hudson Palisades just minutes from the George Washington Bridge, the borough is renowned for its thriving Korean-American community and bustling Broad Avenue commercial corridor. The borough offers an urban feel with convenient access to New York City, a rich culinary scene, and a welcoming atmosphere for residents from around the world.
highlights: Broad Avenue Korean dining and shopping corridor
Minutes from the George Washington Bridge and NYC
One of Bergen County's most ethnically diverse communities
Compact, walkable borough with urban convenience

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. The borough features a mix of single-family homes, multi-family dwellings, and condominiums.
realEstate.recentTrends: Palisades Park has seen steady price appreciation driven by its proximity to NYC and diverse housing stock. The condo market is particularly active, appealing to first-time buyers and investors. Limited land area means new development often involves redevelopment of existing properties.
neighborhoodNotes: Broad Avenue area — Central commercial district with Korean restaurants and shops
Northern section — Quieter residential streets near Leonia border
Southern section — Closer to Fort Lee with larger residential properties
Grand Avenue corridor — Mix of residential and small commercial properties

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Palisades Park Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by neighborhood zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same day as recyclables
Recycling bins available from the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Electronic waste drop-off events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Suez Water (now Veolia) and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia on a quarterly basis
Sewer charges included on property tax bills
Emergency water line repairs available through Veolia's 24/7 hotline

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board of Adjustment
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for all new construction and changes of use

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates municipal parking lots and enforces street parking regulations.
details: Metered parking available along Broad Avenue commercial district
Municipal parking lots located near Broad Avenue
Residential parking permits required in some zones
Alternate side parking enforced for street cleaning
Overnight parking restrictions in effect on certain streets

additionalServices.title: Palisades Park Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library offering books, digital resources, bilingual programs, and community meeting spaces.
details: Located at 257 Second Street
Bilingual programs in English and Korean
Children's story times, teen programs, and adult ESL classes
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer camp programs
Senior citizen programs and activities
Community Center available for events and programs
Annual community events and celebrations

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Palisades Park enforces noise regulations to balance quality of life in this densely populated borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM
Commercial establishments must control noise levels impacting residential areas

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition, including lawns, structures, and sidewalks.
keyRules: Lawns and yards must be maintained and free of debris
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with height limits per zoning district
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Commercial properties must maintain storefronts and signage in good condition

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Palisades Park requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Excessive barking complaints may result in violation notices
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is heavily regulated due to the borough's density.
keyRules: Alternate side parking enforced for street cleaning on posted schedules
No overnight parking on borough streets without a residential permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Commercial vehicles prohibited from parking on residential streets overnight
Metered parking enforced in commercial zones

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy of rental units
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Overcrowding violations enforced based on occupancy limits
Rental property inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Signage Regulations
other.summary: Commercial signage must comply with borough standards for size, placement, and illumination.
keyRules: Signs must be in English or include English translation per borough ordinance
Sign permits required for all new commercial signage
Illuminated signs must not create glare into residential areas
Temporary banner permits required for special events

publicSchools.name: Lindbergh Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school serving younger students in the district

publicSchools.name: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Upper elementary school for the borough

publicSchools.name: Palisades Park Jr./Sr. High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior and senior high school serving all secondary students in the borough

privateSchools.name: Palisades Park Christian Academy
privateSchools.notes: Small Christian school serving the local community

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey City University (Jersey City) — 20 minutes

whyMoveHere: Exceptional Korean and Asian dining scene rivaling NYC neighborhoods
Extremely close proximity to New York City via George Washington Bridge
More affordable than neighboring Fort Lee and Edgewater
Compact, walkable borough with urban amenities

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / George Washington Bridge
commuteToNYC.duration: 20-40 minutes to Midtown Manhattan by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Multiple NJ Transit bus routes provide direct service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. The George Washington Bridge is minutes away for drivers. Many residents also use the Edgewater Ferry for commuting to Manhattan.

costOfLiving.summary: Palisades Park offers relatively affordable housing for its proximity to NYC, though costs are above national averages due to the Bergen County location.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 15% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Outstanding Korean and Asian restaurant scene along Broad Avenue
Very short commute to Manhattan via bus or car
More affordable than many neighboring Bergen County towns
Culturally vibrant with diverse community events
Convenient access to shopping and services
cons: Very densely populated with limited open space
Street parking can be extremely difficult to find
Traffic congestion, especially near the GWB approaches
Schools rated below Bergen County average
Older housing stock in many areas

restaurants.name: So Kong Dong
restaurants.description: Acclaimed Korean restaurant specializing in tofu stew (soon tofu) and traditional Korean dishes, a Broad Avenue institution.

restaurants.name: Bonchon
restaurants.description: Popular Korean fried chicken chain known for crispy double-fried chicken with signature soy garlic and spicy glazes.

restaurants.name: Myung Dong Noodle House
restaurants.description: Casual Korean noodle restaurant serving handmade noodles, dumplings, and savory soups.

restaurants.name: Pal's Cabin
restaurants.description: Korean BBQ spot offering tabletop grilling with premium meats and traditional banchan side dishes.

restaurants.name: Paris Baguette
restaurants.description: Korean-French bakery and cafe offering pastries, cakes, sandwiches, and specialty coffees.

parks.name: MacArthur Park
parks.description: Borough park featuring playground equipment, basketball courts, and open green space for community gatherings.
parks.address: 1st Street & Brinkerhoff Avenue, Palisades Park, NJ 07650

parks.name: Palisades Park Community Center & Field
parks.description: Community recreation area with sports fields, walking paths, and seasonal activities.

parks.name: Palisade Avenue Pocket Park
parks.description: Small green space offering scenic views of the Palisades cliffs and a quiet spot for relaxation.

events.name: Palisades Park Korean Festival
events.description: Annual celebration of Korean culture and heritage featuring traditional music, dance, food, and performances.

events.name: Palisades Park Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Annual Fourth of July celebration with live music, food vendors, family activities, and fireworks.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting ceremony, caroling, and community festivities.

attractions.name: Broad Avenue Koreatown
attractions.description: Vibrant Korean commercial corridor spanning several blocks along Broad Avenue, one of the premier Korean dining and shopping destinations in the eastern United States.

attractions.name: Palisades Interstate Park (nearby)
attractions.description: Stunning cliffside park along the Hudson River offering hiking trails, scenic overlooks, and picnic areas. Accessible from the borough via local roads.

attractions.name: H Mart Palisades Park
attractions.description: Flagship location of the Korean-American supermarket chain, offering an extensive selection of Asian groceries, prepared foods, and specialty items.

shoppingAreas: Broad Avenue — Korean shops, restaurants, bakeries, and specialty stores
Grand Avenue — Small businesses and convenience stores
Fort Lee shopping — Adjacent borough with major retail options
Bergen Town Center (Paramus) — Regional shopping center nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 275 Broad Avenue, Palisades Park, NJ 07650

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
